Transcrição Completa: Mental Attitude with Brandon Carter
[00:00] How many of you guys were here last time I spoke? Show hands. Okay. Those of you who had hands, was it the best or the greatest speech you had ever heard? It was the greatest. Yeah.
[00:15] I figured that. I figured this much. Funniest. All right.
[00:20] Um, I know a lot of people were guys were here giving you different ways to make money, right? Different tactics and strategies and all that shit is great, right? But you know, if your mind's not right, if you don't have the right mental attitude, you won't be able to use that information as effectively. You won't get as much out of it.
[00:40] You know, it's kind of like putting the best fuel in like a shitty car or something like something to that effect, right? That wasn't best example, but you know what the fuck I mean, right?
[00:50] So, I kind of want to talk about some different ways how you can change your fucking mind. Yeah, all of you like change your mind and to get the most out of it and to be more productive and most of all make more money. Is that cool?
[01:05] All right. And then also, I feel like last time I didn't give no like real tactical business shit. How many of y'all have like personal brands that you make money from? Anyone here? Okay. Or you work or you do ads for someone who has a personal brand? There's a lot of you.
[01:20] Can I make a - I make about $2.5 million a year just off my Instagram. We because we track it. I can share that with y'all as a bonus if y'all want to hear it. That doesn't even count all the ads and shit Jeremy and that's just my Instagram. Maybe I'll be a little bonus at the end. Is that cool? Y'all want to know about that?
[01:40] All right. Now, first talk about getting your fucking mind right. So, I got to tell you a story to really illustrate this. I just moved to Miami not long ago, but I used to live in Miami before in around 2007. And who's watching this? Is this being recorded anywhere?
[01:58] A little bit. Okay. All right. No, that's good. That's good to know.
[02:05] So, I was living in Miami. There was this guy. It was a guy named BC and BC was a drug dealer. He sold cocaine professionally and this is a story he told me. This is the story of BC. This legend has it. This is how the story went.
[02:25] He was about to buy a good amount of cocaine from another guy. We'll call him Rodrigo. All right. BC didn't know Rodrigo, right? So, he had - but BC had a - Rodrigo had a mutual friend. It was a real butch lesbian chick. We'll call her Aaliyah. Just like imagine like a dude with titties. Yeah. She was a homosexual. That's cool. Anyway, she was one of the homies.
[02:50] And so she introduced brand. So she was like she said, "Yo, BC, I got the connect for you. I'mma introduce you to him." And BC was like, "All right, cool." So they go to his house kind of late kind of late at night, you know, and it's a normal house. They get in, they meet each other. There's three of them.
[03:10] And they sit down at this table, right? So the table is kind of imagine a table horizontal this way, right? Aaliyah's gay ass. I mean, Aaliyah was sitting here, right? BC was here and Rodrigo was right here. All right, they were sitting down.
[03:25] Rodrigo brings out the cocaine and he's like, "All right, I'mma weigh it for you." And BC was like, "No, I want to use my scale." And Rodrigo was like, "Oh, I want to use my scale." All right, let's both weigh it.
[03:40] So, they agreed to both weigh it and then they get out the money and, you know, it came out to what it was going to be. Then BC brings out the money, asks Rodrigo to count it in front of him just so he knows he don't come back saying crazy.
[03:55] And this whole thing, this whole event took a good amount of time. They were there for a while. You know how long it takes to weigh cocaine, right? I don't have to tell you. And they've been there for a while and then soon as Rodrigo's done counting the money, he said, "Yeah, it's all here."
[04:15] And then in front of BC was a room with a door. That door starts opening really slow. Soon as - and then Rodrigo sees that shit dives on the table to cover all on the cocaine and the money. Aaliyah jumps up out of her seat and BC - BC jumps up and he pulls out a fucking handgun.
[04:35] I know that may not be shocking to you guys. You guys are Florida residents. Who's strapped right now? All of you. Do they give you one when you're born here? Do they hand out the pistol?
[04:48] You just got your ID. Yeah, I just got my Florida ID. Yeah. Concealed weapon. Well, anyway, so BC comes, he pulls out the strap. I'm not going to tell you what happens next. Something happens.
[05:05] But just take a minute right now. Some of y'all are on the edge of your seats, right? You kind of like felt it, right? I want to illustrate something. We're gonna - I'm gonna tell you what happened in the story, right?
[05:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Whatever You Focus On"
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Slide com foco em conceito mental, escrita manual no quadro branco
[05:20] But here's the thing. Whatever you focus on, okay, I mean, my handwriting is - it looks what's the word? Fucked. That was close. That was close. And I have dyslexia. I was diagnosed dyslexia. So you make fun of me for it. It's basically a hate crime and you're a bad person, right?
[05:45] So, oh, I can't spell word. I'mma spell a bunch of words wrong, too. Don't worry about it. When you got fucking enough money, man, you spell shit the way you want. You know what I'm saying?
[05:55] All right. So, here's the thing. Whatever you focus on. I got you guys focusing on this story about our hero BC on his way to - He's just trying to get some cocaine and all this crazy shit has happened. He's just trying to live his life, get some cocaine to distribute it throughout the community and then he's got to deal with all this shit right?
[06:20] So, you focus on this, right? It's like when you watch a movie, you ever watch a movie, you're like, "Oh, shit." You know, you start - whatever you focus on, you're going to feel, right?
[06:30] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Focus → State"
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Diagrama de fluxo simples mostrando relação causa-efeito
[06:32] And that focus and that can be called state, right? Whatever - it's like if whatever you focus on is going to control your mental state, right? It's going to - Yeah. Whatever you focus on, you're going to feel whether it's real or not, right?
[06:50] How many of you guys have pets? All right. So, I want you to imagine your pet real quick. Just walk with me. Imagine your pet. Imagine you're at your home sitting down in your favorite chair and your pet comes up to you slow, looks at you and just dies, right?
[07:10] That's awful, right? Don't worry. Your pets are safe. Nothing happens. It's all make believe. But some of y'all who felt it? Who felt it? You fuckers are lying. You felt that shit. Your dead pets. You felt that shit. But they're alive, right?
[07:28] Whatever you focus on, you're going to feel, right? And it's going to affect your state. And now your state, your state will affect your actions, right?
[07:38] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "State → Actions"
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Continuação do diagrama de fluxo no quadro
[07:40] So, if you're in a fucking shitty state or in a sad state, you're probably not going out in - or at least it's going to be significantly more difficult to go out in the world and crush it in a crush your goals or imagine somebody's in a doing a sales call, but they have a shitty state. How's that going to work for them, sales guy?
[08:02] That's not going to work that great. You know, or whatever you're trying to do, right? If your state is bad, your behavior will be bad and that's going to affect your what? Fuck - your results.
[08:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Actions → Results"
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Completando o diagrama de fluxo: Focus → State → Actions → Results
[08:20] Okay, this thing is hard to write on. No excuses. That's the best I can do. It's going to affect your results, right? So, but it all starts with what you focus on. Does it make sense? You guys are feeling me?
[08:38] Yeah. You wasn't feeling me. You ain't saying - I ain't hear you say nothing. You feel me? Okay. All right. Cool.
[08:48] So, this is important. Whatever you focus on, you're going to feel. Now, here's the thing, though. You might be thinking, big Brandon Carter. The fuck is you talking about? Who cares? Right? Well, this is why it's important because it affects your results.
[09:08] So, how do I do this shit? How do I move this over? Oh, there we go. There we go. All right. Everyone give me a round of applause. I figured that shit out. I did that.
[09:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Mental RAM"
NÚMEROS: 5 anos (sem ficar doente)
VISUAL: Conceito de RAM mental escrito no quadro, analogia com computador
[09:25] Right. So, the problem is with a lot of entrepreneurs in general, especially I think we're plagued with this more than the average civilian. Our focus gets scattered and you could be focusing on all types of different shit that goes on in your mind.
[09:42] How many of you guys have been sick in the last 20 - in the last 12 months? Had a cold, little sniffles. That's almost everybody. Guess when the last time I was sick. Guess was. Yeah. Now I hadn't been sick in about five years. Yeah. You know what I'm saying? I'mma show you why.
[10:08] I have this theory and I think I ain't got no science to back this shit up, but I'm sure you guys can find some because I know it's true. So, some of you one of you who's - you can figure it out. Yeah, you can - you look like you can do research. Exactly. That's a compliment.
[10:28] No, it's a compliment. Can't stereotypes be positive? No. Is that - Was that wrong? Was that offensive that Asian people are good at things? I know. It's so awful. It's like you - someone like you guys all know I'm great at basketball. You can look at it and tell it's not that's not a fuck - I wouldn't be upset about that. You're not going to - Anyway, focus.
[10:55] So, it's something - it's I made this up mental RAM. So when you have a computer, you got a bunch of different programs running, bunch of tabs open, you know, um, shit you got - you got Zoom up, you know how Zoom slows your computer down, you know, Zoom or you know, Pornhub, you got like 30 tabs, right? Excel, right?
[11:18] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Computer Programs = Mental Programs"
NÚMEROS: 30 tabs mencionados
VISUAL: Analogia visual entre abas de computador e processos mentais
[11:20] And you got all this - You got all these tabs open, right? All these programs running. It slows the computer down because the computer only has so much RAM, right? So your brain is the same way, right? It can do a lot of things at once, right? But if you have too many programs open, too much shit going on, what happens is it slows it down.
[11:45] You can't - you - it takes up mental RAM and this is not just business this is life too right because you got to remember it's one CPU so like your business and your personal shit can't be separated.
[12:00] So what happens is you have all these different programs running what happens your cortisol goes up what does cortisol do to your immune system anyone know? Yeah it fucks it up then next thing you know you sick and shit. And you ain't making as much money because you sick am I - Getting sick is stupid as fuck man.
[12:22] It's financially irresponsible to get sick. That's why we got to take this seriously. Um, for example, I have like two laptops and sometimes I'll have like a bunch of shit running on one, but then I'll only be on Zoom on the other computer, right? You can't do that with your life, right?
[12:42] Right? I had two different CPUs, but you can't do that with your with your life. So, as entrepreneurs, we have all the stress of our business and all this other shit right? But then you got all this other your life stuff, right?
[12:58] You have your girlfriends, boyfriends. Um, I didn't mean to point to you like that. I didn't mean that. But if that's - that's just how you - That's cool, too, man. Said it's not the '90s. You can do whatever you want. Your boyfriends um or like your kids and shit you know, um whatever's going on.
[13:20] It's like all this shit starts to take up mental RAM. So the easiest thing to do and what - what the reason that's important because it fucks up your what - your focus now you can't focus is your focus is diverted is is it's is diluted, right?
[13:38] And that and your focus affects your state and your state affects your actions. Your actions affects your results. Next thing you know, you're not making as money as you could, right?
[13:50] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Projects & Conflicts"
NÚMEROS: 2 categorias principais, 8 negócios mencionados
VISUAL: Duas colunas no quadro: Projects | Conflicts
[13:52] So, you got to - you got to - I'm going to give you guys a framework to fix this. I know it works because anytime one of my salespeople - I when they really like - I know these guys are good but when they when they when someone's struggling we look at the numbers they're struggling I pull them aside and I don't really drill them on shit.
[14:15] I - we drill every morning they know what to do what to say shit is easy right but what they - I put them through the same framework where I'm about to put you guys through and they start doing it and next thing you know their numbers go back up and they start making more money.
[14:32] They start making more money, right? They didn't - I didn't make them better salespeople. I didn't give them no new shit. I just lowered - I helped them lower their mental RAM and they made more money and then I made most importantly I made more money as a result.
[14:48] I don't really care about them or their lives, right? If they didn't make me money, they could die tomorrow and I would - I might forget it happened within 48 hours. I'mma be honest with you. Okay. Okay.
[15:02] So, there's two things that fuck up your mental RAM. All right. It's two things. It's ah fuck - Okay. It's projects and conflicts. All right.
[15:15] So, a project will being - some of you guys got like eight businesses and shit you know, you got - or it could be business shit like a bunch of projects in your business or it could be life shit. Maybe you're moving, you know, or you got to find the kids a new school or whatever. Just like things that projects that you have in your brain.
[15:35] This shit takes up mental brain. It's like a pro. I want you think about it like your brain is a computer. It's like a program that's running. And just like your computer, if too many fucking programs are running, it's going to slow it down, right?
[15:50] So projects and then there's conflicts, right? You got might have had beef with people or you know you're having an issue with someone or it could be it could be a spouse or a child or employee employer employee um customers right or it could be with a - it could be with Facebook itself right or PayPal right.
[16:15] Yeah see that's a conflict he needs to address that that's taking a mental RAM this man needs to heal. This man needs to heal.
[16:25] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "E.D.P. Framework"
NÚMEROS: 3 opções de ação
VISUAL: Lista com três letras: E (Eliminate), D (Delegate), P (Plan)
[16:28] All right. So, what you want to do is most of you fuckers ain't going to do it. Only the most - only the strongest among you would do it, right? But you want to make a list of all the projects you have in your life that are taking up mental RAM. Right?
[16:45] So, when I'm with my sales team, with a sales member, I like to do this in private because he might have some shit he don't want to say um in front of the rest of the team. But I - All right, let's make a list of everything. Let's make a list of everything that you feel like is taking up mental RAM in your head, right? And then you make a list of the conflicts, right?
[17:08] And with sales people, man, in - how many of you guys have sales teams? Yeah. You really? All right. And um and you know, you gotta - would you agree with this? I feel like I you might have a different take on this and I'd be interested to hear that.
[17:25] I feel like I have to almost like take charge of their lives like a like a dad, right? Because if they if their shit gets too fucking hectic, I know that it's going to affect with their money. Would you agree with that or - No, that's the best way. That's the best way. Yeah, man. I gotta be daddy. Not like that, man. You know, um.
[17:48] But you want to make a list of all the conflicts you have in your life and all the projects and you're going to do one of three things with these. All right? You're going to put - you're going to put uh a E D or a P next to it.
[18:02] And what this is what it stands for is E stands for eliminate, man. Fuck it. Maybe you could just say, "Man, fuck that shit I ain't gonna do that no more." Right?
[18:15] Like I - I was talking to my man earlier who was who was speaking um he's helping out. I need the list of booty girls. Right. Yeah. Yeah, I need the list, man, because you know I don't sell fajas, man, but I got something for him. You know what I'm saying? I got something for him.
[18:35] All right, I digress. So, maybe - I was telling them earlier, why'd I bring that up? I - Because I was telling him earlier, I had - I had a bunch of different businesses and then one day I just shut all down, but the one I have now and my income actually tripled, like my gross income tripled, right? You know.
[18:58] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Income Tripled After Elimination"
NÚMEROS: 3x income increase
VISUAL: Resultado de eliminar múltiplos negócios e focar em um
[19:00] Um, so there's a lot of shit you could just say, man, fuck that shit. Fuck it. Maybe you just eliminate, don't do it. Or you can put it off, but it's probably easier to just say, "Fuck it." Right?
[19:12] And the D is for delegate. Right? Can you get someone else to do it? Right? This is the reason I I'm really conscious of this shit. This is the reason I don't fucking cook. I don't clean. I don't wash my own clothes. I mean, I'm not dirty. I have someone else do it. I know what you're thinking. No, no.
[19:35] Um, you know, I don't even drive. Like, I don't even - I don't even get a car. I haven't had a car in years. Um, you know, I don't want to think about traffic or where I'mma park that shit. You know, I rather just hit a button on my phone, immigrant picks me up, takes me wherever I want to go, right? I don't have to think about it no more. You know what I'm saying?
[19:58] And so can - can you delegate it or maybe you can't do neither, right? So you have to like plan it. You have to plan it out. And the first thing we do is I don't sit on there and plan their shit with them. It's like they're not they're not literal children.
[20:15] What I what I tell them to do is set a - how long do you think you need to plan this thing? Right. Okay. Then we - we bring up their calendar, Google calendar, and - all right. When you going to do it? Just let's just bar - let's block off that time in your calendar, right?
[20:35] That's one thing I do. I live out a Google calendar as I keep my every fucking hours pretty much accounted for in there, right? And I help them do that, too. It just organizes my life and it lowers my mental RAM because I know what I'm going to do, when I'm going to do it, you know?
[20:52] But anyway, I make them plan when they're going to plan it, right? Just set aside because otherwise, if you just get around to it, you won't. You won't, right?
[21:02] And I'm just telling you, man, just doing these things and setting a time when you're going to plan that shit - I can feel that their state improves. And I asked him, "Yeah, man. I feel better right now." Cuz then you can turn off all that shit at least briefly. You know, you can turn it - it's like you almost feel lighter, right?
[21:22] 📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ───────────────────────────── TEXTO: "Focus → State → Actions → Results"
NÚMEROS: Não mencionados
VISUAL: Retorno ao diagrama de fluxo completo, reforçando o ciclo
[21:25] And then then you - what do you get back? You get back your focus, right? You get back your focus. Now you can you can direct that focus into income generating activities um with more with more um vigor.
[21:40] Everybody feel me right now? You good? All right. Who's going to give this a shot? I'm telling you, this shit will work. This shit will work, man. You'll feel way better and you'll probably like all this shit you got going on, your life will probably improve, too, right?
[22:00] I mean, especially when you start eliminating shit or getting other people to do it or just just plan and all the stuff, you got to just putting a plan to it, a action plan, you'll feel way better.
[22:12] And what's going to happen from that is - see if I can do this, fuck - All right, there we go. Right now, you get your focus back, which would improve your state. You actually feel better, right? Then you'll take better actions and you'll make more money.
[22:32] Any questions? Any questions? I know that was awesome, right? What's up?
[22:40] Oh, finish the story. So, I started with that story just because I wanted to let you show you how what - whatever you focus on you're going to feel. And you know, you kind of felt - you might have felt the -
[22:55] So when BC pulled the gun out, somebody started walking out that door. It was a fucking five-year-old boy with a - holding - he was like he had some sort of animal and he's wearing Transformers shit pajamas. The Transformer movies that came out around that time. You know, he had Transformer.
[23:15] So, you know, BC put the gun away and then Rodrigo was like, "Go back inside. Go back." That's why he - That's why he jumped over the fucking drugs and shit because he didn't want his son to see. Go back inside. The kid said, "Can I have some water?" And he said, "Yeah, I'll get you. Daddy, bring some water." I said, "You stay inside."
[23:38] And I remember - I remember BC thinking, "Man, this is - Look at this, man. We got this kid involved. We - we bad people, the three of us, man." You know, um and BC thought to himself, I'mma stop doing this, man. I'mma stop doing this. Right after we sell - sell the rest of this cocaine, we're not doing this no more.
[24:02] And he didn't. He didn't, man. Things - He turned his life around. From what I hear, things have been pretty well - have been are going pretty well for him now. He's doing pretty well for himself. Um.
[24:18] The guy just jumped all over the cocaine. He jumped - he like dived on it, right? Yeah. He's like - he like covered it up. He's like shirt and shit. Didn't like get on it on it, but he - he didn't like dive on the table, but he was like covering it, but it was like real fast and violent yelling at the door as a baby walks through.
[24:38] It's pretty intense, man. BC thought, "Damn, man. What if he started - what if he fucking let that shit off?" You know, that would have been fucking awful, right? Yeah, that would have been awful. But all's well that ends well. All the cocaine got sold. Rodrigo got his money. The kid got his water.
[24:58] And it's really - it's really a heartwarming story if you think about it. Everybody, every - everybody turned out all right in that story. All right, cool, man. Any questions or are we or are we good? Cuz I know -
📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO
NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:
$2.5 milhões/ano gerados apenas do Instagram
5 anos sem ficar doente
Income triplicou (3x) após eliminar múltiplos negócios e focar em um
48 horas para esquecer funcionários que não geram resultados
ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:
Mental RAM Framework: Analogia entre processos de computador e processos mentais
E.D.P. System: Eliminate (Eliminar), Delegate (Delegar), Plan (Planejar)
Focus → State → Actions → Results: Cadeia de causa e efeito para produtividade
Google Calendar Management: Bloqueio de horários para todas as atividades
Sales Team Mental Management: Gestão do estado mental da equipe de vendas como prioridade
Delegation Strategy: Terceirizar cozinhar, limpar, lavar roupa, dirigir
ESTRUTURA:
Duas categorias de Mental RAM: Projects (Projetos) e Conflicts (Conflitos)
Abordagem de vendas: Estado mental afeta performance de vendas
Liderança paternalista: Assumir controle da vida dos vendedores para proteger resultados
Tracking de receita: Sistema de rastreamento de receita por canal (Instagram separado de ads)
DADOS FINANCEIROS:
$2.5M anuais apenas de Instagram (não inclui receita de ads)
Triplicação de renda bruta após eliminação de negócios paralelos
ROI de terceirização: Eliminar tarefas básicas para focar em income-generating activities
CONCEITOS-CHAVE:
Cortisol e Sistema Imunológico: Excesso de Mental RAM aumenta cortisol e reduz imunidade
Want vs Need: Querer o negócio mas não precisar dele (mentalidade de vendas)
Focus Control: O que você foca determina o que você sente, mesmo que não seja real
Financial Irresponsibility of Illness: Ficar doente é financeiramente irresponsável
DURAÇÃO: ~25 minutos FORMATO: Palestra presencial com quadro branco, storytelling e framework prático
